subject: Re: EER movies processed as UNSELECTED
body:
Hi all,

I actually saw the same issue before, with movies getting marked as
"manually deselected" as the processing went over them one by one. I never
managed to find the actual problem, but I did indeed solve it by doing a
"hard" reset as Dimitry suggested above.

Best,
Lorenzo

On Wed, Oct 5, 2022 at 9:12 PM teg...@gmail.com <tegunov@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi Paul,
>
> Formatting the whole drive is probably too extreme ;-) Removing the old
> installation, the previous.settings file in the processing directory, as
> well as the per-movie .xml metadata and processing results would be the
> hardest reset you can make.
>
> Do you see any error messages in the terminal windows that pop up when you
> click "Start Processing"? Or do they get nuked during processing before
> they can tell you anything?
>
> Cheers,
> Dimitry
>
> On Friday, September 30, 2022 at 12:43:10 AM UTC-7 windy...@gmail.com
> wrote:
>
>> Selected the data folder where the eer movies are located and setup
>> parameters. All eer files can be seen as UNPROCESS, until I click [start
>> processing], from that point one by one each file  becomes  UNSELECTED?
>>
>> I have tried numerous things... uncheck MOTION, uncheck CTF, change
>> parameters, reinstall the 1.0.9 warpm but the behaviour is the same.
>>
>> Weirdly straight after the first installation I just selected the file
>> path and click [start processing]and it was working with the incorrect not
>> optimised parameters.
>>
>> Second installation puzlled in a file from somewhere that was not
>> uninstalled as it remember my previous parameters!
>>
>> Next step is to format the drive and start again.
>>
>> It would be nice to know what the bug is... previous convers mentioned
>> CTF settings?
>>
>> thanks
>> Paul (Imperial college London)
>>
> --
